flashcards.front: What is a Data Model in DBMS?
flashcards.back: A data model is a collection of concepts that describe the structure of a database. It provides an abstraction layer to represent real-world data in a structured format for storage, retrieval, and manipulation.
An entity is a real-world object with independent existence (e.g., employee, product). An entity set is a collection of entities of the same type (e.g., all employees).
An attribute is a characteristic or property of an entity that describes it. For example, Employee entity has attributes: emp_id, name, salary, department.
A key attribute is a unique identifier for an entity. Primary key uniquely identifies each entity. Candidate keys are attributes that can qualify as primary key.
A relationship is an association among two or more entities. For example, 'works in' relationship connects Employee and Department entities.
Cardinality specifies the number of instances of one entity that can associate with a single instance of another entity. Types: One-to-One, One-to-Many, Many-to-Many.
Participation constraint specifies whether the existence of an entity depends on its being related to another entity. Types: Total (mandatory) and Partial (optional) participation.

A weak entity is an entity that doesn't have a primary key of its own and depends on a strong entity for identification. It is identified by combining its partial key with the primary key of its owner.
A composite attribute can be divided into smaller sub-attributes. For example, address can be divided into street, city, state, zip.
A multivalued attribute can have multiple values for a single entity. For example, phone_number can have multiple values. Represented by double oval in ER diagram.
A derived attribute is an attribute whose value can be calculated from other attributes. For example, age can be derived from date_of_birth. Shown by dashed oval.
A binary relationship involves two entity sets. Most common type in ER modeling. Examples: Employee works in Department (1:N), Student enrolls in Course (M:N).
A recursive relationship (also called unary relationship) is when an entity set relates to itself. Example: Employee manages Employee (hierarchical structure).
Data independence is the ability to change schema at one level without affecting the schema at the next higher level. Two types: Physical and Logical data independence.
Schema is the overall description or structure of the database (blueprint). Instance is the actual data stored in the database at a particular point in time.
